headline: Transcript: UK Labour’s Budget borrows big, taxes more
summary: Sonja Hutson talks to Lucy Fisher and Stefania Palma
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘UK Labour’s Budget borrows big, taxes more’

Sonja Hutson Good morning from the Financial Times. Today is Thursday, October 31st. And this is your FT News Briefing.

Microsoft and AI are just two peas in a pod these days. And Chancellor Rachel Reeves announced some bold moves for the UK economy. Plus, in the US battleground state of Arizona, new voter registration rules could disenfranchise some groups. I’m Sonja Hutson. And here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Another day, another blockbuster earnings report for Big Tech. This time we are talking about Microsoft. Its revenue rose 16 per cent last quarter and beat analyst expectations. That’s because of increased demand for cloud computing during this artificial intelligence boom. Microsoft has been benefiting from AI all year. Its share price has increased about 16 per cent since January. But yesterday its shares slipped a bit after the comp
